metadata.label: gen2/Discord-DBInfra
metadata.title: Software Engineer- Database Infrastructure
metadata.team_direction: Discord is a platform used by over 200 million people for gaming communication and interaction

revision_priority: Add genuine Rust evidence or remove this resume from consideration for this JD because Rust is mandatory.
Rewrite one core experience bullet to show explicit database-infrastructure ownership instead of broad backend/data collaboration.
portfolio_review.main_failure_reason: Add genuine Rust evidence or remove this resume from consideration for this JD because Rust is mandatory.

findings.field: TikTok Experience bullet 1, bullet 3, and project bullet 1
findings.issue: Modifiers such as team-maintained and team-owned are bolded, which dilutes emphasis that should be reserved for technologies, scale, and outcomes.
findings.fix: Remove bold from modifiers like team-maintained and team-owned; keep bold only on core tech, scope, and results.

findings.issue: JD must-have Rust is absent from the resume entirely, so the required backend stack is incomplete for this role.
findings.fix: If Rust is real, add it to Skills and support it with one concrete bullet in the most relevant backend/infrastructure experience; otherwise this resume is not a true match for the role.

findings.issue: The latest experience leans on percentage improvements without any Tier 1 scope signal such as request volume, service count, team size, or SLA, so the infra story is weaker than it could be for a senior backend role.
findings.fix: Add one concrete scope metric to the most relevant TikTok bullet, then keep the percentage as secondary evidence.

revision_priority: [MUST] Add Rust evidence if it is genuinely supported by your work, ideally in the most relevant backend or infrastructure bullet.
[MUST] Add one concrete Tier 1 scope signal to the TikTok experience so the latest role reads as scale-aware, not only improvement-aware.
[SHOULD] Remove bold from modifiers like team-maintained and team-owned; keep emphasis on technologies, scope, and outcomes.

Reduce bolding on modifiers such as team-maintained and team-owned and reserve emphasis for technologies and outcomes only.
